blurb: A neighborhood kitchen turning seasonal ingredients into dishes worth returning for.

story.title: Our story
paragraphs: We opened our doors because the food we craved wasn't on the menu anywhere nearby. Today we serve the kind of meals we'd want on our own table — straightforward, generous, and made from scratch.
Our menu changes with the seasons because the best ingredients have a window. When you eat with us, you're tasting what the farms around us are doing right now.

hero.alt: Warm restaurant interior with hanging lights

about.alt: Chef plating a dish at the pass

menu.intro: A small menu that changes with what's in season. Ask us what's good tonight.

items.name: Market Soup
items.description: Whatever's freshest this week, simply prepared and served with our house bread.

items.name: Farm Greens
items.description: Local greens, sherry vinaigrette, shaved hard cheese.

items.name: House Pasta
items.description: Hand-rolled noodles in a sauce that depends on the day. Always good.

items.name: The Steak
items.description: Locally raised, cooked over fire, served simply with a side.

testimonials.quote: Easily the best meal we've had this year.
testimonials.author: A. Friedman

testimonials.quote: Comes back to me at random hours. The pasta especially.
testimonials.author: M. Patel
